body.p1: HobbyHash Coin uses SHA256 mining. The site is built around home miners, hobby miners, solo miners, small mining setups, and simple pool configuration.
body.p2: Solo mining means a miner is trying to find a real block. Accepted shares prove your miner is working, but they are not the same thing as finding a block.
body.p3: Small miners can use the Nano Pool when they need very low share difficulty to show activity. This is useful for nano miner setups, Bitaxe-style devices, NerdMiner-style hobby miners, USB test miners, and other low-hashrate SHA256 hardware.
body.p4: Use the Nano Pool at stratum+tcp://pool.hobbyhashcoin.com:5556 with a worker name like HOBC_ADDRESS.nano1. The pool is still solo only, so accepted shares show valid work but do not guarantee a payout.
body.p5: These ports are present in the current website and pool API configuration. Do not use unconfirmed pool ports from random posts or screenshots.
body.p7: A big share only wins a block if it meets or beats the current network difficulty target. Shares below network difficulty are useful progress signals, but they are not blocks.
body.p8: Current site copy says pool payouts go to the HOBC address before the dot in the worker name. Final payout behavior always depends on the current live pool configuration.
heading.nano_miners_and_small_sha256_dev: Nano miners and small SHA256 devices
hero.lead: Set up HOBC solo mining for home miners, ASICs, and tiny SHA256 devices.
hero.title: Mining Guide
meta.description: HOBC solo mining setup for home and nano miners.
meta.title: Mining Guide
notice.1: Mining is not guaranteed income. Payouts depend on finding a real block under current pool rules.
term.accepted_share: Accepted share: the pool accepted submitted work from your miner.
term.best_difficulty: Best difficulty: the highest-difficulty share seen from a miner or pool over a period.
term.higher_difficulty: Higher difficulty reduces share spam for stronger miners.
term.low_difficulty: Low difficulty helps tiny miners submit shares more often so the dashboard can show activity.
term.main_pool_detail: Main Pool: intended for normal SHA256 ASIC miners and higher-hashrate devices. Current site text lists start difficulty as 5000.
term.nano_pool_detail: Nano Pool: intended for very small miners, test miners, and low-hashrate SHA256 devices. Current site text lists start difficulty as 0.005.
term.network_difficulty: Network difficulty: the chain target a share must meet or beat to become a real block.
term.rejected_share: Rejected share: the pool rejected submitted work. Causes include stale work, wrong settings, bad address format, latency, or miner firmware issues.
term.stale_share: Stale share: work submitted too late, usually after the pool has moved to newer work.
